title: Financial Rate Benchmarking Report
subtitle_COPY: Florida Medicaid · Fee-for-Service Claims · 2024 Analysis Period

bullets: Your organization's H2019 (Behavioral Therapy Services) rate of $143.65 per claim is well above the look-alike peer median of $73.96 (n=10), representing a $87,391 annual rate advantage that warrants investigation into whether billing patterns, session lengths, or modifier usage explain this premium.

Targeted Case Management (T1017) shows a declining rate trend from $59.69 in 2019 to $49.69 in 2024, and caseload per clinician of 179.5 patients falls well below the Similar CMHCs median of 275.0, suggesting a widening productivity gap.

Approximately 49.2% of your organization's visible revenue—spanning Assertive Community Treatment (26.1%) and Crisis Stabilization (23.1%)—has limited or no peer benchmark data available under this methodology.
Medication Management (T1015) holds a stable rate position at $68.84 per claim, above the look-alike peer median of $50.72 (n=10), generating a $10,473 rate advantage worth protecting.

Assessment codes H0031 and H0032, while representing only 2.4% of revenue, both show rates well above peer medians, though each has fewer than 100 claims, so these findings should be interpreted with caution.

section_1_about.org_profile_WRITE: Your organization operates as a comprehensive community behavioral health system with both CMHC and community behavioral health classifications, and is registered as a residential treatment facility. Outpatient Therapy accounts for 40.8% of visible revenue ($497,850.55), followed by Assertive Community Treatment at 26.1% ($318,844.30), and Crisis Stabilization at 23.1% ($281,712.25). Case Management (6.9%), Assessment (2.4%), Psychosocial Rehabilitation (0.6%), and Medication Management (0.1%) make up the remainder.
section_1_about.data_coverage_WRITE: This report benchmarks claims attributed to professional servicing clinicians with a Florida practice location, covering 18,654 total claims and $1,220,424.58 in total revenue across the 2024 analysis period. Your organization is registered as a residential treatment facility, but no residential per-diem codes appear in the data, meaning residential revenue is not captured here.
section_1_about.claim_count_note_COPY: Claim counts in this report reflect claims attributed to professional servicing clinicians with a Florida practice location. Your organization's total claim volume for some codes is higher (e.g., 2,904 total H2019 claims vs 1,254 used for benchmarking) because facility-level and out-of-state claims are excluded from the rate comparison.

four_metrics.rate_per_claim_FIXED: What Medicaid pays your organization, on average, each time you submit a claim for a given service. This is your price signal.
four_metrics.patients_per_clinician_FIXED: How many unique patients each of your billing clinicians serves. This is your caseload signal.
four_metrics.visits_per_patient_FIXED: How many claims are generated per unique patient. This is your treatment engagement signal.
four_metrics.revenue_per_patient_FIXED: Total Medicaid payment received per unique patient for a given service. This is your combined economic signal.

section_1_about.peer_groups_WRITE: Your organization is compared against 10 look-alike peers selected by similarity of visible Medicaid revenue mix, with similarity scores ranging from 0.7639 to 0.8559. In addition, five secondary peer groups provide broader context: All FL Medicaid Orgs, Similar CMHCs, Similar Size Orgs, Similar Market, and Same Classification. The look-alike group identifies peers who bill a comparable blend of outpatient therapy, ACT, and case management codes.
section_1_about.how_to_read_FIXED: In each chart, the diamond marks your organization's value. The blue line marks the peer median. The purple line marks the look-alike peer median. The gray band spans P25 to P75.

badge_legend_FIXED.Strong: Above market median, holding steady or pulling ahead
badge_legend_FIXED.Watch: Above median but converging toward it, or below median but recovering
badge_legend_FIXED.Concern: Below the median on most peer comparisons

section_1_about.key_limitation_FIXED: Rate per claim measures what your organization is paid per claim submission, not per 15-minute unit of service. Organizations that bill longer sessions will show higher per-claim amounts.

section_2_dashboard.coverage_note_WRITE: Assertive Community Treatment (26.1% of revenue) and Crisis Stabilization (23.1% of revenue) have limited or no peer benchmark data available under this professional-NPI methodology, meaning nearly half of your organization's visible revenue cannot be fully benchmarked in this report.

H2019.rate_WRITE: Rate badge: Strong. Your organization's payment per claim of $143.65 is well above the look-alike peer median of $73.96 (n=10) and the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of $71.43 (n=614).
H2019.productivity_WRITE: Productivity badge: Strong. Your organization serves 166.7 patients per clinician, which is well above the Similar CMHCs median of 123.0 and above the Similar Size Orgs median of 147.0.
H2019.utilization_WRITE: Utilization badge: Strong. Your organization generates 2.51 claims per patient, which is at the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of 2.51 but well above the Similar CMHCs median of 2.04.
H2019.revenue_WRITE: Revenue badge: Strong. Your organization's revenue per patient of $360.27 is well above the look-alike peer median of $130.42 and well above the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of $180.81.
H2019.trend_COPY: Rate rose from $96.94 (2019) to $143.65 (2024), pulling further ahead of peers.
H2019.revenue_impact_COPY: $87,391 annual rate advantage vs look-alike peers
H2019.key_question_WRITE: Could longer session durations, modifier patterns, or multi-unit billing be contributing to a per-claim rate that is roughly double the peer median?
H2019.caveat_WRITE: This rate measures what your organization is paid per claim submission, not per 15-minute unit of service; organizations billing longer sessions will show higher per-claim amounts.

T1015.rate_WRITE: Rate badge: Strong. Your organization's payment per claim of $68.84 is above the look-alike peer median of $50.72 (n=10) and near the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of $70.18 (n=550).
T1015.productivity_WRITE: Productivity badge: At Market. Your organization serves 230.0 patients per clinician, which is approximately at the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of 227.0 and the Same Classification median of 230.0.
T1015.utilization_WRITE: Utilization badge: Strong. Your organization generates 1.26 claims per patient, above the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of 1.11.
T1015.revenue_WRITE: Revenue badge: Strong. Your organization's revenue per patient of $86.50 is above the look-alike peer median of $65.70 and above the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of $76.94.
T1015.trend_COPY: Rate rose from $55.98 (2019) to $68.84 (2024), holding a stable position relative to peers.
T1015.key_question_WRITE: Is your organization's above-market rate on T1015 driven by different session lengths or service intensity compared to peers?
T1015.caveat_WRITE: This rate measures what your organization is paid per claim submission, not per 15-minute unit of service, and organizations that bill longer medication management sessions will appear to have higher per-claim rates.

T1017.rate_WRITE: Rate badge: At Market. Your organization's payment per claim of $49.69 is approximately at the look-alike peer median of $49.65 (n=10) and the Similar Market median of $49.69 (n=77).
T1017.productivity_WRITE: Productivity badge: Concern. Your organization serves 179.5 patients per clinician, which is well below the Similar CMHCs median of 275.0 and well below the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of 258.0.
T1017.utilization_WRITE: Utilization badge: At Market. Your organization generates 4.70 claims per patient, which is approximately at the Similar CMHCs median of 4.70 but below the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of 5.57.
T1017.revenue_WRITE: Revenue badge: At Market. Your organization's revenue per patient of $233.80 is approximately at the look-alike peer median of $224.51 and at the Similar CMHCs median of $240.24.
T1017.trend_COPY: Rate declined from $59.69 (2019) to $49.69 (2024), while the statewide median rose — a widening gap.
T1017.key_question_WRITE: Is the low caseload of 179.5 patients per clinician (vs. the peer median of 275.0) a result of staffing changes, panel assignment practices, or a deliberate clinical model choice?
T1017.caveat_WRITE: This rate measures what your organization is paid per claim submission, not per 15-minute unit of service, and the declining trend may reflect changes in session length or coding patterns rather than a true rate reduction.

H0031.rate_WRITE: Rate badge: Strong. Your organization's payment per claim of $57.85 is well above the look-alike peer median of $29.05 (n=8) and the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of $31.59 (n=383).
H0031.productivity_WRITE: Productivity badge: Concern. Your organization serves 76.0 patients per clinician, which is well below the Similar CMHCs median of 115.42 and below the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of 83.0.
H0031.utilization_WRITE: Utilization badge: At Market. Your organization generates 1.20 claims per patient, which is approximately at the Similar CMHCs median of 1.22 but below the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of 1.48.
H0031.revenue_WRITE: Revenue badge: Strong. Your organization's revenue per patient of $69.27 is well above the look-alike peer median of $37.21 and well above the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of $51.80.
H0031.trend_COPY: Rate rose from $45.43 (2019) to $57.85 (2024), diverging upward from peers who remained near $31.
H0031.key_question_WRITE: Is the high per-claim rate driven by longer or more complex assessments, or could modifier usage and rollup be inflating the average?
H0031.caveat_WRITE: This code has only 91 claims in the analysis period, so the per-claim average is sensitive to outliers, and all modifier variations are combined into a single per-code figure.

H0032.rate_WRITE: Rate badge: Watch (Above but Converging). Your organization's payment per claim of $76.81 is above the look-alike peer median of $67.70 (n=10) and the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of $70.33 (n=390).
H0032.productivity_WRITE: Productivity badge: Concern. Your organization serves 33.0 patients per clinician, which is well below the Similar CMHCs median of 75.0 and well below the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of 91.0.
H0032.utilization_WRITE: Utilization badge: At Market. Your organization generates 1.03 claims per patient, which is approximately at the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of 1.02 and the Similar CMHCs median of 1.03.
H0032.revenue_WRITE: Revenue badge: Strong. Your organization's revenue per patient of $79.14 is above the look-alike peer median of $69.31 and above the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of $73.39.
H0032.trend_COPY: Rate declined from $92.19 (2019) to $76.81 (2024), converging toward the peer median of $70.
H0032.key_question_WRITE: Is the converging rate trend a sign that session lengths or service intensity are normalizing, and could this rate continue declining toward the peer median?
H0032.caveat_WRITE: This code has only 68 claims in the analysis period, making the average sensitive to outliers, and all modifier variations are combined into a single per-code figure.

99214.rate_WRITE: Rate badge: Watch (WELL BELOW but Recovering). Your organization's payment per claim of $15.77 is well below the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of $42.75 (n=4,211) but above the look-alike peer median of $13.47 (n=4).
99214.productivity_WRITE: Productivity badge: Concern. Your organization serves 37.0 patients per clinician, which is well below the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of 100.0 and well below the Similar CMHCs median of 98.67.
99214.utilization_WRITE: Utilization badge: Strong. Your organization generates 1.22 claims per patient, which is above the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of 1.11.
99214.revenue_WRITE: Revenue badge: Concern. Your organization's revenue per patient of $19.18 is well below the All FL Medicaid Orgs median of $48.75, though above the look-alike peer median of $17.76.
99214.trend_COPY: Near-zero payments in 2019, rising to $15.77 in 2024, suggesting recent adoption.
99214.key_question_WRITE: Is 99214 a recently adopted service for your organization, and if so, are the low volumes and rates expected to grow as the service matures?
99214.caveat_WRITE: This code has only 45 claims in the analysis period, so rate and productivity figures should be treated as preliminary rather than established patterns.

section_3_deep_dives.h0040_section_WRITE: Assertive Community Treatment (H0040) represents 26.1% of your organization's visible revenue, with $318,844.30 in payments across 11,008 claims at a rate of $28.96 per claim. Only 4 of the 10 look-alike peers bill this code, which severely limits the ability to construct a meaningful peer comparison. Because ACT services are often delivered through team-based models with different billing structures, a dedicated ACT peer group would provide more useful context for evaluating this service line.
section_3_deep_dives.s9485_section_WRITE: Crisis Stabilization (S9485) represents 23.1% of your organization's visible revenue, with $281,712.25 in payments across 484 claims at a rate of $582.05 per claim. No peer benchmark data is available for this code under the current methodology. Crisis stabilization is billed primarily at the facility level rather than through individual professional clinicians, and the benchmarking methodology in this report uses professional-NPI aggregation, which cannot produce a statewide peer distribution for this code. A facility-level analysis using AHCA cost report data would yield a meaningful comparison.

section_4_cross_cutting.caseload_pattern_WRITE: A consistent pattern of low caseloads appears across multiple codes: T1017 shows 179.5 patients per clinician versus the Similar CMHCs median of 275.0, H0031 shows 76.0 versus 115.42, and H0032 shows 33.0 versus 75.0. This pattern could reflect a smaller clinical workforce carrying lighter panels, a deliberate model emphasizing longer or more intensive encounters, or administrative constraints on panel assignment.
section_4_cross_cutting.rate_outlier_WRITE: The H2019 per-claim rate of $143.65 is roughly double the peer median of $73.96, while T1015 at $68.84 and T1017 at $49.69 are both near their respective peer medians. This suggests the rate premium is specific to H2019 billing patterns rather than a systemic organization-wide rate advantage.
section_4_cross_cutting.revenue_concentration_WRITE: The top three service lines—Outpatient Therapy (40.8%), Assertive Community Treatment (26.1%), and Crisis Stabilization (23.1%)—account for 90.0% of visible revenue. Of that total, 49.2% (ACT plus Crisis) has limited or no peer benchmarking available under this methodology, meaning the most actionable comparisons cover just over half of your organization's revenue.

section_5_caveats.peer_methodology_note_COPY: The look-alike methodology matches organizations by the similarity of their visible Medicaid revenue mix. It identifies peers who bill a comparable blend of outpatient therapy, ACT, and case management codes. However, it does not directly match on organizational structure. Your organization operates a crisis stabilization unit (23.1% of visible revenue) and is registered as a residential treatment facility, but most look-alike peers lack these service lines. For crisis and residential services, a service-specific peer group would provide more meaningful comparison.
section_5_caveats.per_claim_vs_per_unit_FIXED: All rate-per-claim figures represent the average payment per claim submission. For time-based codes, a single claim may encompass multiple 15-minute units.
section_5_caveats.modifier_rollup_FIXED: Assessment codes (H0031, H0032) combine all modifier variations into a single per-code figure.
section_5_caveats.residential_gap_WRITE: Your organization is registered as a residential treatment facility, but no residential per-diem codes appear in the claims data, so residential revenue is not captured or benchmarked in this report.
section_5_caveats.volume_limitations_WRITE: Three codes have fewer than 100 claims in the analysis period—H0031 (91 claims), H0032 (68 claims), and 99214 (45 claims)—so their per-claim averages should be interpreted with caution.
